inputs.label: Total Revenue (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Total sales revenue from food sales per month. Standard financial metric for restaurant operations.

inputs.label: Total Food Cost (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Total cost of ingredients and food supplies used in a month. Key input for food cost percentage calculation.

inputs.label: Total Labor Cost (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Total wages, salaries, and benefits for kitchen and service staff. Important for prime cost analysis.

inputs.label: Total Overhead Cost (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Rent, utilities, insurance, and other fixed operating expenses. Based on standard accounting practices.

inputs.label: Waste Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Estimated percentage of food purchased that is wasted. Lean Six Sigma waste reduction metric.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: User's confidence in the accuracy of input data. Adjusts output reliability.

thresholds.foodCostPercentage: if foodCostPercentage > 0.35 then 'High food cost ratio; target < 30%'
thresholds.primeCostPercentage: if (foodCost + laborCost) / totalRevenue > 0.65 then 'Prime cost too high; target < 60%'
thresholds.wastePercentage: if wastePercentage > 10 then 'Excessive waste; investigate processes'

formulas.dataConfidenceAdjusted: if dataConfidence == 'low' then netProfitMargin * 0.9 else if dataConfidence == 'medium' then netProfitMargin * 0.95 else netProfitMargin

suggestedActions: if foodCostPercentage > 0.35 then 'Negotiate with suppliers or adjust menu pricing'
if wastePercentage > 10 then 'Implement inventory management and portion control'
if primeCostPercentage > 0.65 then 'Review labor scheduling and reduce overtime'
